mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show a positive correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in 70% of instances where UNRATE increases exceed 0.2pp, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to generate positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects strengthening supply-side capacity utilization, which boosts corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration correlates negatively with 20-day SPX returns during early-cycle phases, or the mechanism yields a negative Sharpe ratio in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Surging consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in over 65% of high-inflation months, or the threshold signal shows no directional edge in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS increase and UNRATE decrease fails to predict positive 60-day returns in a majority of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly expands corporate earnings potential, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of short-term noise.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day SPX forward returns across multiple rolling windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained breakdown in industrial production below its medium-term trend signals structural supply-side weakness and impending earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO drops below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive in 70% of cases when INDPRO falls below its 6-month average, or the signal fails to achieve a positive information coefficient.
